title: Spicy Silky Algerian Sauerkraut Tagine
description: A spicy and silky Algerian dish featuring white sauerkraut, bhuna, and Jain-friendly ingredients, perfect for a quick and flavorful meal. This tagine is a unique blend of North African and Indian flavors.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large tagine or Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the diced red chili peppers and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Stir in the bhuna curry powder and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the canned crushed tomatoes, vegetable broth, and salt. Stir to combine.
Add the white sauerkraut and stir to combine. Bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 20-25 minutes, or until the sauerkraut is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro and serve hot over rice or couscous.
